title: Archival Preservation of Cell Suspensions for scRNA-Seq
author: Linas Mazutis

steps.title: Using the protocol of your choice,...
steps.instruction: Using the protocol of your choice, dissociate the solid tissue sample into a single-cell suspension.

steps.title: Resuspend the dissociated cells in 400...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the dissociated cells in 400 µl cell culture media (e.g RPMI, DMEM) supplemented with 2.5% FBS and 0.1 mg/ml DAPI dye. Aim for dilution of 500-2000 cells/µl. Incubate on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: Add 400 µl of Bambanker preservation...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µl of Bambanker preservation media in a 15 ml FACS collection tube. FACS-sort DAPI negative (live-cells) directly into the preservation media. Collect >200,000 cells ideally, or 50,000 cells at a minimum. NOTE: The preservation medium will get diluted by the FACS buffer during sorting. Avoid diluting more than 50%.

steps.title: Transfer the FACS-sorted cell suspension into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the FACS-sorted cell suspension into a 1.5 ml LoProtein Bind tube, but do not exceed 500 µl volume. If the sorted suspension constitutes more than 0.5 ml, then divide the solution into two tubes (e.g. each tube 400 µl). Transfer the tubes to a swinging bucket centrifuge and spin down at 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes NOTE : For centrifugation in a swinging bucket centrifuge place 1.5 ml tubes inside a 50 mL Falcon tube. After centrifugation is complete, remove the medium without disrupting the pellet leaving ~20 µl of supernatant in the tube. Dilute the cells with the preservation medium down to 1000-2000 cell/µl. Use the FACS quantification as a guide, but keep in mind that these numbers are often inflated. Gently resuspend the cells using a 1000 µl or 200 µl pipette. Do not leave cell clumps behind. At this step, one would typically expect to have 50-100 µl of preservation medium containing sorted live cells (at a concentration of 1000-2000 cells/µl). NOTE: When resuspending the cells in preservataion medium, make sure to avoid bubbles and do not leave any cell clumps.

steps.title: Use slow freezing method (–1°C/minute) to...
steps.instruction: Use slow freezing method (–1°C/minute) to freeze the cells at −80 °C. Place the tube containing cells into a isopropanol-filled container and transfer it in a –80 °C freezer. Isopropanol-filled containers have a stated freeze rate of –1°C/minute, but performance may vary. The next day, transfer the tube to liquid nitrogen tank for a long term storage.

steps.title: CRITICAL
steps.instruction: CRITICAL: When thawing cells, it is of critical importance to proceed quickly, within 5-10 minutes. Handling thawed cells for longer than 15 min may result in poor quality data. Therefore, make sure that the scRNA-seq platform is set up and reagents are ready before starting the thawing procedure. Remove the tube from liquid nitrogen and transfer to water-bath set at 37 ºC Incubate the tube for 1-2 minutes, during which the frozen cells should thaw completely.

steps.title: Count the thawed cells by mixing...
steps.instruction: Count the thawed cells by mixing 5 µl of cell suspension with 5 µl of 0.1% (w/v) Trypan Blue.

steps.title: Evaluate cells under bright field microscopy...
steps.instruction: Evaluate cells under bright field microscopy (or alternatively, using the Countess II instrument).

steps.title: When using 10X Genomics Chromium system...
steps.instruction: When using 10X Genomics Chromium system to perform scRNA-Seq follow the "Cell Suspension Volume Calculator Table" in the CG000204 RevD manual here .

steps.title: Load the cells directly onto a...
steps.instruction: Load the cells directly onto a chip and place the chip into the Chromium instrument.

steps.title: Proceed according to 10X Genomics protocol ( here )
steps.instruction: Proceed according to 10X Genomics protocol ( here ).

steps.title: Follow the 10X Genomics manual CG000204...
steps.instruction: Follow the 10X Genomics manual CG000204 RevD here to construct the scRNA-Seq library.

steps.title: We use a NovaSeq 6000 instrument...
steps.instruction: We use a NovaSeq 6000 instrument to sequence the final DNA libraries using pair-end sequencing option, R1 read - 26 cycles, R2 read - 70 cycles, and index read - 8 cycles, aiming for ∼150M reads per ~5,000 single-cells.

steps.title: We process FASQ files using the...
steps.instruction: We process FASQ files using the SEQC pipeline and mapped to a reference genome with the default SEQC parameters to obtain the gene-cell count matrices.

steps.title: and incubated in liquid nitrogen for 2 weeks
steps.instruction: and incubated in liquid nitrogen for 2 weeks. Next, cells were thawed, counted and processed for scRNA-Seq (steps 6-9). The results are summarized in Figures 1-2 below: Figure

steps.title: Comparison of gene expression profiles of...
steps.instruction: Comparison of gene expression profiles of fresh and frozen single cells derived from human lung. tSNE plot of all cells (grey) derived from a fresh human lung sample, colored by whether they were suspended in preservation medium before freezing (blue), previously frozen in preservation medium (orange), or never frozen cells suspended in RPMI 1640 medium (green). Figure

steps.title: Comparison of cell types in fresh and previously frozen samples
steps.instruction: Comparison of cell types in fresh and previously frozen samples. Color bars indicate proportions of cell types. Y-axis, percentage of cells; X-axis, Phenograph cluster numbers corresponding to distinct cell types. Red, dissociated cells suspended in preservation medium and processed immediately; blue, dissociated cells frozen in the preservation medium and processed after 2 weeks in liquid nitrogen; purple, dissociated cells resuspended in RPMI medium and processed immediately.
